title: In /Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config, verify branch/commit existence, whether commit 1c8023c7 is on feature/phase0-handoff-pipeline, and whether there is evidence it was pushed. Use local git evidence only unless remote check is necessary. Return concise findings. Do not edit files.
summary_text: **Minimal viable attestation (practical and defensible now):** - **Introduce a single signed evidence envelope** as the authoritative proof format for every provider lane run in: - `scripts/lib/kernel-codex-run-driver.sh` (or equivalent codex launch path), - `scripts/lib/kernel-glm-exec.sh`, - `scripts/lib/kernel-optional-lane-exec.sh`. Fields should be minimal: `run_id`, `provider`, `attempt_id`, `command_hash`, `prompt_sha256`, `exit_code`, `started_at`, `finished_at`, `output_summary`, and `evidence_id`; JSON string is signed with `openssl` HMAC (`KERNEL_EVIDENCE_HMAC_KEY`) or SHA-256 over canonical JSON. - **Write evidence to an append-only file location** per run (e.g., `${state_root}/provider-evidence/<run_id>/<evidence_id>.json`) and have producers be the only writers in these scripts, with `0700` dir and file perms. - **Switch acceptance in ledger to evidence-first mode**: - `scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh` adds `record-provider-evidence <evidence_file>` (or `record-provider` accepts file-only in strict mode), - verifies HMAC, validates `provider` matches expected lane, verifies `command_hash` against known invocation context, checks evidence freshness/nonce/attempt monotonicity, deduplicates by `evidence_id`, then records a verified success/failure. - keep direct `record-provider` as test-only behind explicit `KERNEL_LEDGER_MANUAL_EVIDENCE=true`. - **Derive health/consensus from verified evidence only**, not raw counters: - `scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh` computes codex/glm/specialist success from verified ledger entries only, - `scripts/lib/kernel-consensus-evidence.sh` requires the same verified evidence predicates before approval. - **Add a one-line integrity check in lane launcher**: - each lane wrapper includes expected script hash + binary path in evidence so tampering with wrapper invocation is detectable in verification. **What this still does not solve:** - It does **not** prevent a fully compromised runner/admin from forging evidence or exfiltrating the HMAC key; threat then becomes out-of-scope of local trust model. - It does **not** prove semantic quality of provider outputs, only authenticated execution attempt/proof. - It does **not** stop semantic-level prompt substitution in a compromised upstream caller; that is a separate integrity channel you would need from upstream signing/orchestration manifests.
